step_by_step_reasoning: Based on the transcript, users upload PDFs to S3. S3 sends events via EventBridge ('main bus'), which triggers a Step Functions state machine. Step Functions orchestrates several serverless steps: first, a Lambda converts pages into images; second, a SageMaker real-time endpoint analyzes the layout using a custom ML model; third, a managed text extraction API (Textract) extracts the text; and finally, a Lambda handles post-processing (reading order, semantic analysis) and publishes a completion event back to EventBridge to notify other services.

graph.name: Docebo Shape Serverless Document Processing and Noise Reduction Pipeline
graph.notes: Handles 10-20 complex documents a day, processing 30-50 pages per minute. Successfully reduces document noise by about 80% to generate bite-sized e-learning courses.

nodes.name: E-Learning Creators
nodes.notes: Upload business documents, scientific papers, or presentations.

nodes.name: Input S3 Bucket
nodes.notes: Stores uploaded raw PDFs and intermediate converted images.

nodes.name: Main Event Bus
nodes.notes: Handles decoupled asynchronous communication across the application.

nodes.name: Processing Orchestrator
nodes.notes: State machine that manages the lifecycle and sequencing of document clean-up tasks.

nodes.name: Page-to-Image Converter
nodes.notes: Converts each page of the document into a separate image.

nodes.name: SageMaker Real-Time Endpoint
nodes.notes: Hosts custom ML model to recognize page structure and layout.

nodes.name: Amazon Textract
nodes.notes: Extracts text content and raw structures from documents.

nodes.name: Post-Processor
nodes.notes: Validates reading order, performs semantic analysis, and filters noise.

edges.notes: Sends page images for layout analysis.
Notifies the creator system that clean document is ready.
